TEMPORARY SPACE | PORTABLE GALLERY is a collectively organised art show in Burgess Park, taking place sometime over the summer with the aim of rethinking institutional art structures through the methods of public art.

We plan to establish a week long show in Burgess Park, using a PORTABLE SPACE that allows us to generate a day-by-day TEMPORARY GALLERY for artists to perform in, hang work and build around. Inspired by the occupation of art institutes in the “Sessantotto” (1968) and the work of “Anarchitecture” (1974) in New York, we look to rethink not just the ‘white cube’ but also the gallery, its walls, permanence and authority over art culture.

This is an open invitation to artists who would like to be involved, to join us in not just the show itself, but helping organise and rethink the gallery. Meetings will be held in person to organise the show and discuss its themes and structure.

Our first meeting will be a walk through Burgess Park on Tuesday 28th of April from 6-8pm. We urge you to join if you can and if you know anyone who may also be interested, please send this on.
